title: Substrings
description:
How many distinct substrings does a given string $S$ have?

For example, if $S =$ abc, $S$ has $7$ distinct substrings: , a, b, c, ab, bc, abc. Note that the empty string and $S$ itself are considered substrings of $S$.

On the other hand, if $S =$ aaa. $S$ has only $4$ distinct substrings: , a, aa, aaa.
input: The first line of the input file contains $N$, the number of test cases. For each test case, a line follows giving $S$, a string of from $1$ to $5000$ alphanumeric characters.
output: Your output consists of one line per case, giving the number of distinct substrings of $S$.
source: Olympiad, Canadian Computing Competition & Olympiad, 2003, CCC 2003 Senior Division
